title: About
text: My name is Noora Mohamed, I am a junior at the University of Nebraska, Lincoln. I double major in Sports media and Journalism, with a minor in Communication studies and Textile, Merchandising fashion design. I recently started my masters in Integrated media Communication at UNL.

title: Who was the better offensive coach in Real Madrid
text:
The results of a game, always comes back to what the coaches teach behind closed doors. Coaches are expected to train, discipline and prepare their athletes for every possibility in the field.
Fans focus on the winning and scoring, the real impact all comes back to the objective, planning and knowledge, that coaches implant in their players.
The scores of a single game, leads back to what the coach taught and how successfully the players executed that vision, revealing the ability the coach has when it comes to playing offense.
Álvaro Arbeloa-
Álvaro Arbeloa, a Spanish former defender football player professional footballer and current manager. Arbeloa has won many trophies; Club World Cup, a UEFA Super Cup, a LaLiga Title, two Copas del Rey and a Spanish Super Cup. Shifting from a player into a coach, Arebola alongside his passion and management that highlighting his career.

Code
madrid <- read_csv("LALIGA25-26.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) |> filter(Pts >0)|> mutate(season="25-26")
madrid1 <- read_csv("LALIGA24-25.csv",show_col_types = FALSE)|> filter(Pts>0)|>
mutate(season="24-25")
madrid2<- read_csv("LALIGA23-24.csv",show_col_types = FALSE)|> filter(Pts>0)|>
mutate(season="23-24")


New names:
• `` -> `...1`


Code
madrid_all<-bind_rows(madrid,madrid1,madrid2)
ggplot(madrid_all,aes(x=reorder(Name, RK), weight=Pts, fill=season))+
 geom_bar(position="dodge")+
             coord_flip()+
      labs(title = "How the changing of coaches has an effect on ranking",
       subtitle = "Nacho ranking was highest overall between season 23-26",
       x = "Change in Ranking number ",
       y = "Name of Team Players",
       caption = "Source: ESPN|By Noora Mohamed")+
  theme_minimal()+
theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"))

Code
ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data=madrid25, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="red") + 
  geom_line(data=madrid24, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="hotpink") +
  geom_line(data=madrid23, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="blue") + 
  labs(title = "How has the scoring changed over time",
       subtitle = "LALIGASEASON",
       x = "GOALS FOR",
       y = "GAMES AGAINST",
       caption = "Source: ESPN")

Code
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork) 
gamess <- read_csv("25_26.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
gamesss<- read_csv("23_24.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
bar1 <- ggplot() + 
  geom_col(data=gamess, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Position),y=LALIGA), fill="blue") + geom_col(data=gamesss, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Position),y=LALIGA), fill="red") + coord_flip() + 
  labs( x="Points", y="Team Ranking") + 
  theme_minimal() 
bar2 <- 
  ggplot() + geom_col(data=gamesss, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Wins),y=LALIGA), fill="darkgrey") + 
  geom_col(data=gamess, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Wins),y=LALIGA), fill="pink") + coord_flip() +
  labs( x="Points", y="Team Ranking") + 
  theme_minimal() 
bar1/bar2 + 
  plot_annotation( title = "Real Madrid LALIGA ranking", 
  subtitle = "During the LALIGA 2023 season Real Madrid was ranked number one by 95 points. Following the 2026 season, Real Madrid is now ranked second, with Barcelona taking the lead by 76 points.",
  caption = "Source: Yahoo Sports | By Noora Mohamed" ) & theme ( 
  plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
  axis.title = element_text(size = 8), plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), panel.grid.minor = element_blank() )
